First, count all equilateral triangles with perimeter at most 20. Then, count all isosceles triangles with exactly two equal sides, and add them together. This way, I avoid confusion and double-counting.

Now, moving on to isosceles triangles with exactly two equal sides. These can be represented as (a, a, b), where a ≠ b, and a and b are positive integers.
